howItWorks.intro: This tariff refund (drawback) calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Eligible Duty = Total Duties × Re-export Percentage
Refund = Eligible Duty × Drawback Rate (99% for manufacturing/unused)
Net Duty = Total Duties Paid - Refund
Effective Rate = Net Duty ÷ Import Value × 100%
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is duty drawback?
faq.answer: Duty drawback is a refund of up to 99% of customs duties paid on imported goods that are subsequently exported, either in their original form or after manufacturing. It's one of the oldest trade facilitation programs.

faq.question: Who qualifies for duty drawback?
faq.answer: Any US importer who re-exports imported goods or products made from imported materials. You have 5 years from importation to file a drawback claim. The goods must be exported within 5 years.

faq.question: How long does it take to receive a drawback refund?
faq.answer: Standard processing takes 3-12 months. Accelerated payment (for qualified claimants) can provide refunds within 2-3 weeks. Filing electronically through ACE speeds up processing.

faq.question: Is the Tariff Refund (Drawback) Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Tariff Refund (Drawback) Calculator?
sections.body: The Tariff Refund (Drawback) Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your import value, total duties paid, percentage re-exported, you get instant results including eligible duty amount, estimated refund, net duty after refund. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting eligible duty amount right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your import value and need to find the right eligible duty amount. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Import Value ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your import value — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: total duties paid, percentage re-exported, drawback type. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your eligible duty amount, estimated refund, net duty after refund. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your import value before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
